test.note: All Milestone A feature tests pass (125+ new tests). Pre-existing fixture-dependent failures in internal/jxr, internal/kfx (pipeline_test.go, kfx_test.go), and internal/scan are expected — KFX fixture files don't exist in the repo.

blockingIssues.description: parentTemplateID not propagated in story branch (yj_to_epub_content.go:~724). processPageSpreadStoryBranch always passes nil as parentTemplateID to child recursive calls. Python passes parent_template_id on the first child, then None for subsequent children. The locID variable is computed but discarded (_ = locID).
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Pass locID as parentTemplateID for the first child (idx==0), then nil for subsequent children.

blockingIssues.description: parentTemplateID not propagated in connected pagination branch (yj_to_epub_content.go:~801). Same bug as story branch — processPageSpreadConnectedBranch always passes nil to children.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Pass locID as parentTemplateID for the first child, then nil for subsequent children.

blockingIssues.description: Incomplete scale_fit condition (yj_to_epub_content.go:~672). Go only checks !cfg.IsPdfBacked to fall to leaf. Python requires is_pdf_backed AND '$67' not in page_template AND '$66' not in page_template. When isPdfBacked is true but $67/$66 are present, Go incorrectly enters scale_fit instead of leaf branch.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Add check for $67/$66 absence: when cfg.IsPdfBacked is true, also verify $67 and $66 are not in pageTemplate before entering scale_fit branch.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: when porting Python functions that use first-iteration vs subsequent-iteration patterns (e.g., parent_template_id on first child, None on rest), workers must explicitly track iteration index and branch on idx==0. The pattern appears in yj_to_epub_content.py:232-233 (page-spread story branch) and line 317 (connected pagination branch).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature a4 missed this pattern in two separate branches. The Python code uses 'for child in children: ... parent_template_id = None' after the first call.

rejectedObservations.observation: Library note state-organize-fragments.md inaccurately claims per-source lastContainerID matches Python's per-container processing
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a documentation accuracy issue in an existing library note, not something the scrutiny validator should modify — library notes belong to the workers who created them

rejectedObservations.observation: makeTestSource helper in state_test.go is unused and could be documented as reusable test utility
rejectedObservations.reason: Low priority — the helper may be used in future integration tests

rejectedObservations.observation: Go's chooseFragmentIdentity has no direct Python equivalent and is undocumented
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a design decision already covered in the library note; not actionable for scrutiny

rejectedObservations.observation: The feature description for a2 had incorrect priority ordering
rejectedObservations.reason: The code and tests have the correct ordering; the feature description is historical and not worth correcting

rejectedObservations.observation: go-port-worker skill RED/GREEN pattern not strictly followed
rejectedObservations.reason: Not actionable for scrutiny — skill accuracy improvements are outside milestone scope

rejectedObservations.observation: maxInt helper should be in shared helpers file
